ViewModel.cells: Array<'X' | 'O' | null> of length 9 (mirrors Board layout)
ViewModel.statusText: string — short outcome/turn message ('Your turn', 'Computer thinking...', 'You win!', 'Computer wins!', 'Draw', etc.)
ViewModel.winningLine: Array<number> of 3 cell indices when a win has occurred, otherwise null
ViewModel.disabled: boolean — true when the board should not respond to clicks (game over or AI mid-move)

render.description: Idempotently updates the DOM to reflect viewModel. Safe to call repeatedly with the same input. Applies winning-line CSS class when winningLine is non-null. Marks board non-interactive when disabled is true.

onCellClick.description: Registers a single handler invoked when a user clicks an empty, enabled cell. Cell index is the 0..8 row-major position. Replaces any prior handler (last-registration-wins) — Builder may choose to support multiple, but a single replaceable handler is the minimum contract.

onRestart.description: Registers a handler invoked when the user activates the restart control.

mount.description: Optional initialization step that injects the renderer's DOM nodes into a host element (defaults to document.body or a designated #app element — Builder picks and documents). Called once by the controller during page load.

guarantees: Renderer holds no game logic: it does not detect wins, it does not validate moves, it does not advance turns. It is a pure projection of viewModel onto DOM.
Hover state on empty cells is purely a CSS concern; no JS hover handlers required.
Renderer does not import rules-engine or ai-opponent.

notes: Controller composes the viewModel each frame: it pulls cells from the GameState's board, decides statusText based on whose turn it is and any Outcome, passes through winningLine from checkOutcome, and sets disabled=true on terminal state or while waiting for the AI. The controller is the only writer; the renderer is the only DOM toucher.
